summary: PR 2734 split the former `melee/gm/gm_1B03.c` range into smaller game-mode translation units, linked most of the split units, adjusted symbols/splits/configuration, and moved or exposed related game-mode data/prototypes. The bot report shows linked code and linked/matched data improvements, while the large list of `gm_1B03` broken matches appears to be the expected consequence of moving functions into new split units.

key_files.role: Updated split ranges for `gm_1B03.c` and added new ranges for `gm_1B0FB.c`, `gm_1B0FF.c`, and `gm_1B14.c`; also adjusted nearby item data split ranges.
Adjusted symbol names/scopes/sizes around the split, including local compiler-style labels for jump tables/string data and local sbss scopes.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:symbols.txt gm_804D68C0/C1/C8 scope and size changes

key_files.role: Changed build matching status: `gm_1B03.c`, `gm_1B0FB.c`, and `gm_1B0FF.c` are `Matching`; `gm_1B14.c` remains `NonMatching`.
Shrank original TU by removing later game-mode scene/functions into new split files; retained early range and declared/moved several data objects.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:gm_1B03.c TODO possible split due to gm_80480DC8

key_files.role: New split unit for the `0x801B0FB8..0x801B0FF8` range; bot report lists `gm_801B0FB8` as newly matched.
New split unit for the `0x801B0FF8..0x801B14A0` range; configured as matching and bot report lists `gm_801B0FF8`, `gm_801B13B8`, and data as newly matched.
New split unit for the `0x801B14A0..0x801B2BA4` range; configured as nonmatching but contains many functions reported as newly matched or improved.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report new matches and unmatched improvements

key_files.role: Header cleanup and extern additions for game-mode globals, including `gm_804771C4` and `gm_80477738`.
Moved `TmData gm_804771C4` definition from static header into C file.
Changed game-mode type surface; exact type additions are not visible in loaded diff excerpt.
Item code adjusted, likely to satisfy data/split linkage side effects; exact edits are not visible in loaded diff excerpt.
Small item code adjustment accompanying item data split range changes; exact source edits are not visible in loaded diff excerpt.
Small library-side adjustment; exact edits are not visible in loaded diff excerpt.

what_changed: Split `melee/gm/gm_1B03.c` text/data ranges into four units: `gm_1B03.c`, `gm_1B0FB.c`, `gm_1B0FF.c`, and `gm_1B14.c`.
Updated `configure.py` so the first three split units are built as `Matching`, while `gm_1B14.c` is still `NonMatching`.
Moved a large tail of code and scene data out of `gm_1B03.c`; the diff excerpt shows removal of `gm_801B0FB8`, `gm_801B0FF8`, scene arrays, and much later code from the original file.
Adjusted symbol metadata around `.data`, `.bss`, `.sdata`, and `.sbss`, including replacing some semantic/generated jumptable names with local `@NN` labels.
Moved `gm_804771C4` from `gm_18A5.static.h` into `gm_18A5.c` and declared it in `gm_18A5.h`.
Added new headers/dox files for split game-mode units and updated includes/prototypes in adjacent game-mode files.
Adjusted item data split ranges for `itmsbomb` and `itflipper`, with related edits in item source files.
smart_moves: Kept split metadata, build configuration, symbol ownership, and source moves in the same PR, which made the split coherent enough for the bot to report linked-code and data improvements.
Marked `gm_1B14.c` as `NonMatching` even though many functions newly matched, preserving the remaining nonmatching status instead of overstating the unit as fully matching.
Used local `@NN` labels for some data/jumptable symbols where the excerpt does not show semantic evidence, aligning with conservative naming rather than inventing meaning.
Moved a real data definition (`gm_804771C4`) out of a static header into a C file and exposed it through the header, reducing header-owned storage risk.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When splitting a formerly large TU, expect per-unit match reports to show old-unit broken matches alongside new-unit matches; interpret these as relocation/split effects only when the same functions appear as newly matched in the new units.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report 46 broken matches in gm_1B03

decomp_lessons.lesson: A split PR can improve linked and matched data significantly even if headline matched-code percentage drops slightly, because symbol/split ownership changes may move code and data across unit boundaries.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report matched code -0.05%, linked code +0.11%, matched data +5.44%, linked data +5.37%

decomp_lessons.lesson: For partially linked split work, keep the not-yet-fully-matching split unit explicitly `NonMatching` in configuration while allowing already-matching smaller split units to become `Matching`.

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style game-mode names such as `gm_801B0FB8`, `gm_803DD888_Scenes`, and `gm_804D68B0` were retained where stronger semantic names are not shown in the PR evidence.
Local data labels may use compiler-style `@NN` symbols instead of speculative names when semantic ownership is not proven in the evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Split by address ranges in `splits.txt`, then mirror those ranges in `configure.py` objects so the linker and match accounting attribute functions/data to the new units.
Adjust symbol scopes/sizes and local labels during a split to match new unit ownership, especially for `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sbss` symbols crossing old boundaries.

Use bot match report deltas to distinguish genuine regressions from expected old-unit broken matches after code moves; in this PR, many `gm_1B03` broken matches correspond to new matches under `gm_1B0FB`, `gm_1B0FF`, or `gm_1B14`.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: GALE01 report showed matched code 74.94% (-0.05%, -1752 bytes), linked code 48.55% (+0.11%, +4440 bytes), total data +3 bytes, matched data +5.44% (+65859 bytes), linked data +5.37% (+65035 bytes). It listed 52 new matches, 46 broken matches, 2 improvements in unmatched items, and 2 regressions in unmatched items.
No human review comments or PR body rationale are present in the loaded evidence.

Claims about exact source edits in `gm_1B0FB.c`, `gm_1B0FF.c`, `gm_1B14.c`, item files, `types.h`, and `lbdvd.c` are limited because their full hunks are not visible in the excerpt.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Split-induced broken matches can be expected when functions move to new units
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2734, the bot report listed many broken matches under old unit `main/melee/gm/gm_1B03` while listing corresponding new matches under split units `gm_1B0FB`, `gm_1B0FF`, and `gm_1B14`. For split PR review, compare old-unit broken matches against new-unit matches before treating them as regressions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Partially matching split units should remain NonMatching in configure.py
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2734 configured `gm_1B03.c`, `gm_1B0FB.c`, and `gm_1B0FF.c` as `Matching`, but left `gm_1B14.c` as `NonMatching` despite many new matches and improvements, reflecting remaining nonmatching work in that split unit.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report gm_1B14 new matches and unmatched improvements

source_update_candidates.title: gm_1B03 split boundaries from PR 2734
source_update_candidates.text: `src/melee/gm/gm_1B03.c` was split in PR 2734 into `gm_1B03.c` (`0x801B0348..0x801B0FB8`, data `0x803DD6A0..0x803DD888`), `gm_1B0FB.c` (`0x801B0FB8..0x801B0FF8`, data `0x803DD888..0x803DD8B8`), `gm_1B0FF.c` (`0x801B0FF8..0x801B14A0`, data `0x803DD8B8..0x803DD9A0`), and `gm_1B14.c` (`0x801B14A0..0x801B2BA4`, data `0x803DD9A0..0x803DDC58`).
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoped history for future work near these split boundaries; curator should verify against current source before promoting.

source_update_candidates.title: Review split PRs by correlating split/configure/symbol changes with match-report movement
source_update_candidates.text: When a PR splits a TU, review the split ranges, configure matching status, symbol ownership, and bot new/broken match lists together. Old-unit broken matches may be expected if the same functions become new matches in newly split units.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential reusable review guidance supported by this PR, but should be curated as a standard only if corroborated by additional split PRs or current project practice.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_1B14 remained configured NonMatching after PR 2734
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2734 added `src/melee/gm/gm_1B14.c` and configured it as `Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gm_1B14.c")`, while the bot report showed many functions in the unit newly matched and two unmatched-item improvements.
source_update_candidates.reason: Helpful historical context for future `gm_1B14` matching work; curator should check current status before promoting.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that the PR fully matched `gm_1B14.c`; `configure.py` explicitly kept it `NonMatching`.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale beyond merge state; no human review comments are loaded.
Do not promote exact lessons about the item or library edits because the loaded diff excerpt does not show their source changes.
Do not treat the existing `#pragma dont_inline` visible in `gm_1B03.c` as a new tactic from this PR; the excerpt does not show it being added.
